sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: The Beneficiary has made considerable scholarly contributions to the field of communication and market research by publishing academic articles in various professional journals. Details can be found in **Exhibits D**.

sections.heading: 1. The Beneficiary's Co-authored Study on Chinese Online Shopping Published in the International Journal of Consumer Studies [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary co-authored the scholarly article "Online shopping among Chinese consumers: an exploratory investigation of demographics and value orientation," published in the International Journal of Consumer Studies. The author listing confirms the Beneficiary as a co-author alongside "Lingfei Wu, Yi Cai, Dehuan Liu." [Exhibit D1, p.2] The article was "First published: 27 January 2011" with a DOI registered through Wiley, one of the world's largest academic publishers with over eight million articles across 1,600 journals. [Exhibit D1, p.2]

The study "was based on responses from a sample of 1620 respondents in five cities in China" and employed logistic regression analysis and structural equation modelling to investigate the relationship between personal value orientations and online shopping behaviour. [Exhibit D1, p.3] The International Journal of Consumer Studies "provides an international forum for academic and research papers with a focus on how consumers can enhance their security and well being" and publishes "articles of interest to an international audience and at the leading edge of consumer research throughout the world." [Exhibit D2, p.3]

Dr. Lingfei Wu, Assistant Professor at the School of Computing and Information at the University of Pittsburgh, whose "research has been published in prestigious journals, including Nature and PNAS," confirmed the article's exceptional citation impact. [Exhibit D3, p.3] Dr. Wu stated that "this paper has been cited 59 times to date, placing it in the top 10% of papers published in this journal over the past fifteen years" and that "[t]hese citations come from various fields, such as market research, information science, economics, and others." [Exhibit D3, p.2]

sections.heading: 2. The Beneficiary's Co-authored Study on Educational Inequalities Published in Sociological Studies [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary further authored the article "Children Born in July and August: A study on the age regulation of primary school admission and student's education access and development," published in Sociological Studies in 2015. [Exhibit D4, p.2] The article was co-authored with Xuelian Li, with the Beneficiary listed as first author: "Author: Dehuan Liu, Xuelian Li." [Exhibit D4, p.2] The printed journal page confirms the article's publication in Sociological Studies, listing the authors as "Liu, Dehuan Li, Xuelian" with keywords including "Educational equity; adolescent relative age effect; adjustment crisis." [Exhibit D5, p.2]

The article's abstract reveals that "students born in July and August show the lowest possibility of getting into the key senior middle schools, which is 16.7% - 22.8% lower than the rest of the student population, while their possibility to get into vocational school is the highest, 54.2% - 60.8% higher than the others." [Exhibit D4, p.2] This research was conducted as part of the "National Social Science Foundation's key project 'Research on the influence and guidance of online games on adolescent development', subject number 11AZD112" and "was funded by the National Social Science Foundation." [Exhibit D4, p.3]

Sociological Studies "is a first-level professional academic journal sponsored by the Institute of Sociology, Chinese Academy of Social Sciences" that "has been ranked first in the subject rankings by four journal evaluation institutions, including the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, Nanjing University, Peking University, and Wuhan University, and is hailed as an 'authoritative journal.'" [Exhibit D6, p.2] The article has attracted 4,896 readers on the journal website, and Professor Liping Sun of Tsinghua University confirmed that the article "attracted intensive attraction from the academic circle, society and government" and that "Professor Xie Yu from Princeton University and many other scholars were inspired by the article and further analyzed educational equality." [Exhibit G12, p.3]

sections.heading: 3. The Beneficiary's Co-authored Study on Advertising Communication Published in Advertising Panorama [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
The Beneficiary also authored the article "New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS," published in Advertising Panorama. [Exhibit D7, p.2] The author listing confirms the Beneficiary as first author: "Author: Dehuan Liu, Siluo Chen." [Exhibit D7, p.2] The article appeared in "Advertising Panorama: General Edition, Issue No. 4, 2013, Pages 96-98" and is classified under Economics Management - Advertising (F713.8). [Exhibit D7, p.2]

In the article, the Beneficiary described the ISMAS model as representing a fundamental paradigm shift, writing that "the core of the consumer market has completely changed, and the marketing model is undergoing a Copernican-style revolution." [Exhibit D8, p.2] The article's citation network demonstrates substantial scholarly impact, with 44 works in its cited literature, 144 second-level cited works, and 271 co-cited works. [Exhibit D7, p.2]

Advertising Panorama is "a professional journal of advertising, marketing and communication approved by the General Administration of Press and Publication of the People's Republic of China" and is "the only professional journal of advertising marketing and communication in China published in ten-day periodicals." [Exhibit D9, p.3]

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Therefore, the Beneficiary's authorship of these highly recognized scholarly articles, their publication in professional journals of international and national standing, and their broad influence and citations across multiple fields all serve to demonstrate that he meets the requirements of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(vi). His influential contributions continue to impact the field, thereby indicating that **the Beneficiary possesses extraordinary ability and is one of the small percentage who have risen to the very top of the field of communication and market research**.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Dr. Liu has made considerable scholarly contributions to the field of sociology by frequently publishing academic articles in various journals. Details can be found in **Exhibits D**.

sections.heading: 1. Dr. Liu's Co-first-authored Study on Chinese Online Shopping Published in the *International Journal of Consumer Studies* [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
Dr. Liu co-first-authored the scholarly article titled, "Online Shopping Among Chinese Consumers: an exploratory investigation of demographics and value orientation." According to Wiley[^7], the article showcases original research exploring the relationship between Chinese consumers' personal value orientations and their online shopping behavior. The research presented in this article represents significant advancements in the understanding of internet usage and consumer behavior among Chinese consumers, contributing to the field of sociology, market research, and information science. **[Exhibits D-1; Information regarding Dr. Liu's Article from *Wiley Journals*]**

The article was published in the *International Journal of Consumer Studies* in January 2011, a reputable scholarly journal with a global readership. Based in the UK, the journal is recognized for publishing research at the leading edge of consumer research worldwide. Given the journal's international reach and its focus on pioneering consumer research, it serves as a highly esteemed platform for scholarly discourse among learned persons in the field. **[Exhibits D-2; Introduction of *International Journal of Consumer Studies*]**

The publication's contribution to the field is further evidenced by its substantial citation count. According to Google Scholar, the paper has been cited 59 times to date, placing it in the top 10% of papers published in the *International Journal of Consumer Studies* over the past fifteen years. The paper's influence is cross-disciplinary, as indicated by its citations across various fields such as market research, information science, economics, and others. **[Exhibits D-3; Dr. Lingfei Wu's Letter]**

[^7]: Wiley Journals: Firmly established as the world's leading society publisher, Wiley offers an exceptional portfolio of over 8 million articles from 1,600 journals.

sections.heading: 2. Dr. Liu's Co-first-authored Study on Educational Inequalities Published in *Sociological Studies* [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
Among Dr. Liu's various scholarly publications, the article, "Children Born in July and August: A study on the age regulation of primary school admission and student's education access and development," co-first-authored with Xuelian Li and published in 2015, stands as a pivotal study in the realm of educational equity. This article emphasizes the inequalities induced by the current primary school admission system, particularly the relative disadvantage faced by students born in July and August. The work is unequivocally scholarly, as it reports on original research within the sociology domain and is written by Dr. Liu, an acknowledged expert in the field. **[Exhibits D-4 & D-5; Description of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Sociological Studies* & First Page of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Sociological Studies*]**

The article was published in *Sociological Studies*, a top-tier professional academic journal under the auspices of the Institute of Sociology, Chinese Academy of Social Sciences. This journal has garnered significant recognition and influence in the sociological field since its inception in 1986. Between 2012 and 2021, *Sociological Studies* was awarded the title of "China's Most Internationally Influential Academic Journal" for nine consecutive years. It has also received accolades such as "China's Top 100 Journals" and "2016 Top 100 Digital Impact Journals." These awards and recognition signify the high circulation and prestige of the journal, as well as the credibility of its intended audience, which includes professional sociologists, researchers, and scholars in the field of sociology, both nationally and internationally. **[Exhibits D-6; Introduction of *Sociological Studies*]**

sections.heading: 3. Dr. Liu's Co-first-authored Study on Advertising Communication Published in *Advertising Panorama* [Exhibit D]
sections.content:
Dr. Liu co-first-authored the scholarly article titled, "New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS," which was published in April 2013. This article exemplifies a profound discourse on the evolution of advertising and communication strategies in the wake of the internet era. The work critically analyzes and interprets major shifts in marketing and consumer behavior influenced by developments in internet technology, thus contributing significantly to scholarly literature in sociology and advertising. **[Exhibits D-7 &D-8; Description of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Advertising Panorama* & First Page of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Advertising Panorama*]**

The article was published in *Advertising Panorama: General Edition*, an authoritative professional journal on advertising, marketing, and communication, distributed both in China and overseas. Approved by the General Administration of Press and Publication of the People's Republic of China, the journal's high status and recognition is well-established. Its readership encompasses core marketers, managers of advertising agencies, employees of media and advertising production companies, as well as academicians from related institutions.[^8] **[Exhibits D-9; Introduction of *Advertising Panorama*]**

The article meets the standards of a scholarly piece as defined in the academic arena. The authors, including Dr. Liu, present an original philosophical discourse on how the advent of the internet has challenged the traditional concepts of information asymmetry in businesses. The article has been co-cited 271 times and cited 44 times, demonstrating its value to other scholars in its field of expertise. **[Exhibits D-7 &; D-8 Description of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Advertising Panorama* & First Page of Dr. Liu's Article Published in *Advertising Panorama*]**

[^8]: People's Daily Online, founded on January 1, 1997, is a publicly traded media and culture company. It has more than 563 million readers on various media platforms, including its websites, mobile apps, and social media platforms, among other formats.

Therefore, Dr. Liu's authorship of this highly recognized scholarly article, its publications in professional journals, and its broad influence and citations across multiple fields all serve to demonstrate that he meets the requirements of 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(vi). His influential contributions continue to impact the field of sociology, thereby indicating that he possesses an "extraordinary ability" within his field.

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(vi), the submitted exhibits establish that the Beneficiary has authored scholarly articles in recognized academic and professional publications in his field [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Publication Record and Journal Credentials
sections.content:
The Beneficiary's publication record includes, among other works, the article "Online shopping among Chinese consumers: an exploratory investigation of demographics and value orientation," published in the International Journal of Consumer Studies on January 27, 2011, as well as the article "Children Born in July and August: A study on the age regulation of primary school admission and student's education access and development," published in Sociological Studies, and the article "New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS," published in Advertising Panorama in 2013 [Exhibit X - x]. These publications were not placed in obscure or informal outlets, but rather in journals and periodicals with established editorial standing and professional reach [Exhibit X - x]. The International Journal of Consumer Studies is published by Wiley-Blackwell Publishing Ltd. and is identified as an international forum for academic and research papers concerning consumer security, well-being, policy, and education, with an H-index of 88 [Exhibit X - x]. Sociological Studies was first published in January 1986, is sponsored by the Institute of Sociology of the Chinese Academy of Social Sciences, is a core journal in the Chinese Social Science Citation Index, and is described as an authoritative journal that employs a three-round anonymous review system [Exhibit X - x]. The exhibits further show that Sociological Studies was awarded the title of "China's Most Internationally Influential Academic Journal" for nine consecutive years from 2012 to 2021 and received additional top-journal distinctions, confirming that the Beneficiary's work appeared in one of the leading sociology journals in China.

The Advertising Panorama materials likewise identify that periodical as a professional journal of advertising, marketing, and communication approved by the General Administration of Press and Publication of the People's Republic of China and open to the public at home and abroad [Exhibit X - x]. The exhibits also show that Advertising Panorama serves a substantial professional readership, has a maximum circulation of 50,000 copies, and is directed to core marketing personnel, advertising executives, media institutions, universities, and scholars [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Measurable Scholarly Impact
sections.content: The exhibits also demonstrate measurable impact from the Beneficiary's published work [Exhibit X - x]. In particular, Professor Lingfei Wu states that the Beneficiary's paper "Online Shopping Among Chinese Consumers: An Exploratory Investigation of Demographics and Value Orientation" was subsequently published in the International Journal of Consumer Studies and "has been cited 59 times to date, placing it in the top 10% of papers published in this journal over the past fifteen years" [Exhibit X - x]. That evidence is especially probative because it does not merely show publication, but also shows that the article performed at a level surpassing the overwhelming majority of papers in that venue [Exhibit X - x]. The Advertising Panorama citation-network exhibit similarly shows that the Beneficiary's article "New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS" has 44 cited-literature references, 271 co-cited-literature entries, and 144 second-level cited-literature entries, reflecting identifiable scholarly engagement with the publication [Exhibit X - x]. The Sociological Studies article further addresses a substantial social-science problem—educational inequality arising from school-admission cutoff dates—and analyzes effects on self-identity, study ability, key-school access, and vocational-school placement, thereby demonstrating the substantive and research-based nature of the Beneficiary's scholarship [Exhibit X - x]. Taken together, these exhibits show not only authorship, but also that the Beneficiary's articles have attracted attention and use within the broader scholarly and professional community [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Expert Peer Testimony
sections.content: Professor Lingfei Wu's recommendation letter provides especially persuasive peer testimony regarding both the quality of the Beneficiary's scholarship and its interdisciplinary influence [Exhibit X - x]. Professor Wu is an Assistant Professor in the School of Computing and Information at the University of Pittsburgh, where he focuses on the science of team science and innovation and uses big data analysis, complexity sciences, and AI in his research [Exhibit X - x]. His profile further states that his research has been published in prestigious journals including Nature and PNAS and that he has received distinctions including the NSF Career Award, the Richard King Mellon Award, and the Oxford Martin School Fellowship [Exhibit X - x]. Against that background, his assessment carries substantial weight.

sections.heading: Cross-Disciplinary Influence and Breadth
sections.content: The breadth of the Beneficiary's influence is also evident from the subject matter and readership reflected in the exhibits [Exhibit X - x]. The International Journal of Consumer Studies article concerns consumer behavior, internet usage, demographics, and value orientation, placing the Beneficiary's work at the intersection of consumer studies, economics, and social research [Exhibit X - x]. Professor Wu's letter confirms that this article has in fact been cited in market research, information science, economics, and other fields, showing cross-disciplinary uptake [Exhibit X - x]. The Sociological Studies publication addresses educational equity, adolescent development, and social inequality, thereby extending the Beneficiary's scholarly reach into sociology and education policy [Exhibit X - x]. The Advertising Panorama article addresses advertising communication, internet technology, information sharing, and consumer behavior, and the associated periodical materials show dissemination to marketing professionals, agency executives, scholars, and media institutions [Exhibit X - x]. In sum, the exhibits show that the Beneficiary's authorship is not limited to a single sub-specialty, but instead reflects published scholarship with academic, professional, and interdisciplinary relevance [Exhibit X - x].

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.content: Accordingly, the record satisfies 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(vi) because it demonstrates the Beneficiary's authorship of scholarly articles in the field in professional or major trade publications and other major media [Exhibit X - x].
